definition: A set of interrelated components that work together and influence one another.
A group of organisms that can interbreed and produce fertile offspring.
The role of a species in an ecosystem, including how it uses resources and interacts with others.
The addition of a substance or agent to the environment at a rate faster than it can be rendered harmless.
The capacity of an area to generate renewable biological resources and absorb wastes.
The maximum population size of a species that an environment can support over time.
The process of change in species composition in an ecosystem over time.
Using resources in a way that allows natural systems to continue functioning for the future.
